name: Formal Verification
description: Formal verification mathematically proves a system meets its specification: a formal model and a property (often in temporal logic) are built, and either model checking explores the state space or theorem proving derives a proof; a failed check yields a counterexample that refines the model until the property is guaranteed.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["System + specification"]
    N2["Build formal model"]
    N3["Express property"]
    N4{"Choose technique"}
    N5["Model checking"]
    N6["Theorem proving"]
    N7["Explore state space"]
    N8{"Property holds?"}
    N9["Produce counterexample"]
    N10["Refine model/spec"]
    N11["Proof / guarantee"]
    N12["Verified system"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 -->|model check| N5
    N4 -->|prove| N6
    N5 --> N7
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|no| N9
    N9 --> N10
    N10 -->|refine| N2
    N8 -->|yes| N11
    N11 --> N12

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N12 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Model checking: algorithmic verification and debugging
sources.authors: Clarke, E. M.; Emerson, E. A.; Sifakis, J.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.
